intent: Write a market intelligence report on the 'Home & Kitchen' product category by triangulating THREE sources: (1) Shopping site (customer-facing) — find the top 5 products in Home & Kitchen sorted by rating, recording each product's name, price, rating, and review count. (2) Shopping Admin (backend) — access the admin dashboard to find any sales/order volume data or inventory information for home & kitchen products. (3) Reddit /f/personalfinance or /f/AskReddit — search for posts where users discuss home purchases, kitchen upgrades, or budget product recommendations. The report must: (a) present the top products from the storefront with price and rating, (b) add backend context from admin (order trends, stock levels if visible), (c) cross-reference with Reddit discussions on real consumer behavior and budget priorities, (d) conclude with a gap analysis — what products are highly rated but under-discussed (or vice versa)? Cite all data points.
author_notes: 3-site task: shopping frontend + shopping admin backend + reddit. Hardest task — requires login to admin, cross-referencing 3 different data types (product catalog, backend metrics, community opinions). DeerFlow should massively outperform single-agent here.
